message.content:
Explore the Crevia MVP codebase at /home/antonypeter/Projects/crevia/creviamvp. I need a deep understanding of the "workspace" feature. Find:
1. All files/components related to "workspace" (pages, components, routes)
2. What the workspace displays and what actions are available
3. How workspace data is fetched (Supabase queries, RPC calls, etc.)
4. Any workspace-related database tables or types
5. Navigation into/out of workspace

Search broadly — check src/pages, src/components, src/app, any routing files, and any hooks or context providers that mention workspace. Report file paths, line numbers, and a clear summary of what the workspace does end-to-end.

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1 import { useState, useEffect } from "react"; 2 import { supabase } from "@/integrations/supabase/client"; 3 import { Button } from "@/components/ui/button"; 4 import { motion } from "framer-motion"; 5 import CreviaChat from "@/components/crevia-connect/shared/CreviaChat"; 6 import { Badge } from "@/components/ui/badge"; 7 import { 8 FileSignature, 9 Receipt, 10 CheckCircle2, 11 Clock, 12 ArrowRight, 13 Sparkles, 14 } from "lucide-react"; 15 16 const DEAL_STAGES = [ 17 { id: "negotiating", label: "Negotiating" }, 18 { id: "contract_signed", label: "Contract Signed" }, 19 { id: "invoice_paid", label: "Invoice Paid" }, 20 { id: "complete", label: "Complete" }, 21 ]; 22 23 const CreviaWorkspace = () => { 24 const [activeStage, setActiveStage] = useState("negotiating"); 25 const [contracts, setContracts] = useState([]); 26 const [invoices, setInvoices] = useState([]); 27 const [loading, setLoading] = useState(true); 28 29 useEffect(() => { fetchData(); }, []); 30 31 const fetchData = async () => { 32 const [{ data: contractsData }, { data: invoicesData }] = await Promise.all([ 33 supabase.from("contracts").select("id, title, client_name, status, value, currency").order("created_at", { ascending: false }).limit(3), 34 supabase.from("invoices").select("id, invoice_number, client_name, total, currency, status").order("created_at", { ascending: false }).limit(3), 35 ]); 36 setContracts(contractsData || []); 37 setInvoices(invoicesData || []); 38 if (invoicesData?.some((inv: any) => inv.status === "paid")) setActiveStage("complete"); 39 else if (invoicesData && invoicesData.length > 0) setActiveStage("invoice_paid"); 40 else if (contractsData?.some((c: any) => c.status === "signed")) setActiveStage("contract_signed"); 41 else setActiveStage("negotiating"); 42 setLoading(false); 43 }; 44 45 const activeIndex = DEAL_STAGES.findIndex(s => s.id === activeStage); 46 47 return ( 48

content.text: Let me read the CreviaStudio page to understand the overall context:
Excellent! Now let me check the CreviaChat component more thoroughly to understand the messaging system:

Perfect! Now I have a comprehensive understanding. Let me create a final summary report of my findings:

## Comprehensive Workspace Feature Report

Based on my deep exploration of the Crevia MVP codebase, here is a complete understanding of the workspace feature:

### 1. **Files and Components Related to Workspace**

**Pages:**
- `/home/antonypeter/Projects/crevia/creviamvp/src/pages/WorkspacesList.tsx` (Lines 1-144): List view of all workspaces
- `/home/antonypeter/Projects/crevia/creviamvp/src/pages/WorkspacePage.tsx` (Lines 1-258): Individual workspace detail page with chat and action vault

**Components:**
- `/home/antonypeter/Projects/crevia/creviamvp/src/components/studio/workspaces/StudioWorkspacesHub.tsx` (Lines 1-495): Main hub for workspace/chat management within Crevia Studio
- `/home/antonypeter/Projects/crevia/creviamvp/src/components/studio/workspaces/WorkspaceInboxList.tsx` (Lines 1-743): Left sidebar with workspace and DM list with CRUD operations
- `/home/antonypeter/Projects/crevia/creviamvp/src/components/studio/workspaces/WorkspaceActionVault.tsx` (Lines 1-554): Right sidebar showing contracts and invoices within a workspace
- `/home/antonypeter/Projects/crevia/creviamvp/src/components/studio/workspaces/StudioIconNav.tsx` (Lines 1-50): Icon navigation for studio tabs
- `/home/antonypeter/Projects/crevia/creviamvp/src/components/crevia-connect/shared/CreviaWorkspace.tsx` (Lines 1-190): Standalone workspace view with deal tracker
- `/home/antonypeter/Projects/crevia/creviamvp/src/components/crevia-connect/shared/CreviaChat.tsx` (Lines 1+): Core chat component that powers messaging within workspaces

**Routing:**
- `/home/antonypeter/Projects/crevia/creviamvp/src/App.tsx` (Lines 124-125):
  - Route: `/crevia-workspace` → WorkspacesList page
  - Route: `/crevia-workspace/:id` → Individual WorkspacePage

---

### 2. **What Workspaces Display and Available Actions**

**WorkspacesList Page (Lines 1-144 of WorkspacesList.tsx):**
- Grid display of all active workspaces
- Shows: Workspace name, last updated date, member count, "Contract" and "Invoice" indicators
- Actions:
  - "New Workspace" button to create workspace
  - Click card to open workspace
  - Shows empty state if no workspaces exist

**WorkspacePage (Lines 1-258 of WorkspacePage.tsx):**
- **Deal Tracker** (4 stages): Negotiating → Contract Signed → Invoice Paid → Complete
  - Automatically progresses based on contract/invoice status
  - User can manually click stages to view/change
- **Main Chat Area** (CreviaChat component)
  - Full messaging interface with file uploads, voice notes, reactions
  - Message types: text, file, voice, invoice, contract, workspace_invite
- **Action Vault** (right sidebar on desktop, mobile sheet):
  - Section 1: Contracts - shows up to 2 contracts with "Sign Contract" button
  - Section 2: Invoices - shows up to 2 invoices with status-dependent actions
  - Quick Actions: Links to "All contracts" and "All invoices"

**StudioWorkspacesHub (Lines 1-495 of StudioWorkspacesHub.tsx):**
- **Pane 1 (Left)**: WorkspaceInboxList - lists all workspaces and DMs
- **Pane 2 (Center)**: 
  - For workspaces: Shows list of workspace members with "Chat" button to open DM with member
  - For DMs: Shows chat interface
  - "Propose Workspace" button in DMs to invite person to a workspace
- **Workspace Members Display**: Shows avatar, display name, handle, role (admin/member)

**WorkspaceInboxList (Lines 1-743 of WorkspaceInboxList.tsx):**
Actions available:
- Create new workspace (Lines 168-188)
- Rename workspace (Lines 190-210)
- Delete workspace (Lines 212-226)
- Start direct message (Lines 243-306)
- Context menu on each workspace (Lines 449-473): Rename, Delete
- Search workspaces/DMs (Lines 308-317)

**WorkspaceActionVault (Lines 1-554 of WorkspaceActionVault.tsx):**
Actions:
- Add member to workspace (Lines 94-113)
- Create contract (Lines 478-484)
- Create invoice (Lines 487-492)
- Sign contract (Lines 269-280)
- Update invoice status (Lines 156-163)
- Delete contract (Lines 165-173)
- Delete invoice (Lines 175-184)

---

### 3. **Workspace Data Fetching (Supabase Queries)**

**WorkspacesList.tsx (Lines 23-30):**
```typescript
supabase.from("chat_rooms")
  .select("*, chat_room_members(user_id, role)")
  .order("updated_at", { ascending: false })
```

**WorkspacePage.tsx (Lines 125-128):**
```typescript
// Parallel fetches:
supabase.from("chat_rooms").select("*").eq("id", id).single()
supabase.from("contracts").select("*").order("created_at", { ascending: false }).limit(3)
supabase.from("invoices").select("*").order("created_at", { ascending: false }).limit(3)
```

**StudioWorkspacesHub.tsx:**
- Line 69-82: Fetch room by ID when navigated from notification
- Line 95-107: Fetch workspace members and their profiles
- Line 133-145: Check for existing DM between users
- Line 157-161: Create new DM room

**WorkspaceInboxList.tsx (Lines 104-166):**
- Fetch all rooms user is member of (chat_room_members)
- Select all chat_rooms with members
- Enrich with profile data for DM partners

**WorkspaceActionVault.tsx (Lines 99-112):**
- Fetch current workspace members (Lines 99-104)
- Fetch all available users excluding current members (Lines 106-111)

---

### 4. **Database Tables and Types**

**chat_rooms** (`/src/integrations/supabase/types.ts` Lines 521-549):
```typescript
Row: {
  avatar_url: string | null
  created_at: string
  created_by: string (user ID)
  id: string
  is_group: boolean
  name: string | null
  updated_at: string
}
```

**chat_room_members** (Lines 489-519):
```typescript
Row: {
  id: string
  joined_at: string
  role: string ("admin" or "member")
  room_id: string (FK to chat_rooms)
  user_id: string
}
```

**chat_messages** (Lines 377-458):
```typescript
Row: {
  content: string | null
  contract_id: string | null (FK to contracts)
  created_at: string
  deleted_for_everyone: boolean
  file_name: string | null
  file_size: number | null
  file_type: string | null
  file_url: string | null
  id: string
  invoice_id: string | null (FK to invoices)
  is_encrypted: boolean
  message_type: string ("text", "file", "voice", "invoice", "contract", "workspace_invite")
  reply_to_id: string | null (FK to chat_messages)
  room_id: string (FK to chat_rooms)
  sender_id: string
}
```

**contracts** (Lines 551-630):
- title, client_name, client_email, status, value, currency, created_at, user_id
- client_signature, creator_signature for signing

**invoices** (referenced but full type not shown):
- invoice_number, client_name, total, currency, status, created_at

---

### 5. **Navigation In/Out of Workspace**

**Navigation INTO Workspace:**
1. **From WorkspacesList** (Line 95): Click workspace card → navigates to `/crevia-workspace/:id`
2. **From CreviaStudio Chat Tab** (Line 159 of CreviaStudio.tsx): Displays StudioWorkspacesHub, click workspace in sidebar → displays workspace members
3. **From DM in Studio** (Line 391 of StudioWorkspacesHub.tsx): Click "Chat" button on workspace member → opens DM with that member
4. **From Notification** (Lines 67-83 of StudioWorkspacesHub.tsx): `initialRoomId` param auto-selects room

**Navigation OUT of Workspace:**
1. **WorkspacePage**: 
   - Back arrow (Line 162): Returns to `/crevia-studio?tab=chat`
   - Quick action buttons (Lines 101-106): Navigate to contracts/invoices tabs

2. **StudioWorkspacesHub**:
   - Mobile back button (Lines 230-239): Returns to inbox list
   - DM "Back to workspace" button (Lines 286-297): Returns from DM to workspace members view
   - Navigation via tab change in header

**Workspace Lifecycle:**
- Create: "New Workspace" button creates chat_room (is_group=false) + adds creator as admin member
- Delete: Removes chat_room_members then chat_room (Lines 212-226 of WorkspaceInboxList.tsx)
- Propose: Special message type "workspace_invite" sent to DM (Lines 181-190 of StudioWorkspacesHub.tsx)

---

### 6. **End-to-End Workspace Flow**

1. User lands on `/crevia-workspace` (WorkspacesList) or accesses via CreviaStudio "Workspace" tab
2. User creates workspace → Creates chat_room (is_group=false, name specified) + chat_room_members entry
3. Workspace appears in list, user clicks to open `/crevia-workspace/:id` (WorkspacePage)
4. WorkspacePage displays:
   - Deal Tracker at top (4 stages)
   - CreviaChat component for messaging
   - Action Vault sidebar with contracts/invoices
5. Messages sent to chat_room are stored in chat_messages with message_type field
6. Contracts/invoices can be attached to chat_room via message_type field + contract_id/invoice_id
7. Workspace members can be added via WorkspaceActionVault "Add" button
8. Member management:
   - Query chat_room_members to get list
   - Join user profiles to display member info
   - Support "Propose Workspace" to invite users from DMs
9. User navigates out via back button or sidebar navigation

The workspace feature is a **professional "deal room"** concept where creators and brands can collaborate on contracts and invoices within an encrypted chat interface, with automatic deal progress tracking.
